```{r}
     return(ggplot() + theme_void() + annotate("text", x=0.5, y=0.5, label="Gene not found\nin this dataset") + ggtitle(GOI) + labs(subtitle=data_source_label))
  }

  if (inherits(data, "DESeqDataSet")) {
      if (is.null(sizeFactors(data))) data <- estimateSizeFactors(data); expr_values <- counts(data, normalized = TRUE)[GOI, ]; y_axis_label <- "Normalized Counts"
  } else if (is(data, "SummarizedExperiment")) {
      expr_values <- assay(data)[GOI, ]; y_axis_label <- "Normalized log2 Intensity"
  }

  plot_df <- data.frame(expression = expr_values, condition = colData(data)$condition)
  plot_df$condition <- factor(plot_df$condition, levels = master_order)
  other_conditions <- setdiff(master_order, ref_group)

  annotation_info <- list(); y_offset <- diff(range(plot_df$expression, na.rm = TRUE)) * 0.05
  if (!is.finite(y_offset) || y_offset == 0) y_offset <- max(abs(plot_df$expression), na.rm=TRUE) * 0.05
  
  if (is(dep_results, "SummarizedExperiment")) { results_metadata <- as.data.frame(elementMetadata(dep_results)); goi_idx <- which(results_metadata$name == GOI)[1]
    if (length(goi_idx) > 0 && !is.na(goi_idx)) { for (cond in other_conditions) { p_col_name <- paste0(cond, "_vs_", ref_group, "_p.adj")
        if (p_col_name %in% names(results_metadata)) { p_adj <- results_metadata[goi_idx, p_col_name]; signif_label <- get_significance_label(p_adj)
          if (signif_label != "ns" && signif_label != "") { max_y <- max(plot_df$expression[plot_df$condition == cond], na.rm = TRUE); annotation_info[[cond]] <- data.frame(condition = cond, y_pos = max_y + y_offset, label = signif_label) }
        }
    }}
  } else if (is(dep_results, "list")) {
    for (cond in other_conditions) { res_name <- paste0("genotype_", cond, "_vs_", ref_group)
      if (res_name %in% names(dep_results)) { res_obj <- dep_results[[res_name]]
        if (is(res_obj, "DESeqResults") && GOI %in% rownames(res_obj)) { p_adj <- res_obj[GOI, "padj"]; signif_label <- get_significance_label(p_adj)
          if (signif_label != "ns" && signif_label != "") { max_y <- max(plot_df$expression[plot_df$condition == cond], na.rm = TRUE); annotation_info[[cond]] <- data.frame(condition = cond, y_pos = max_y + y_offset, label = signif_label) }
        }
      }
    }
  }
  annotation_df <- bind_rows(annotation_info)

  p <- ggplot(plot_df, aes(x = condition, y = expression)) +
    geom_boxplot(fill = plot_color, alpha = 0.6, color = NA, outlier.shape = NA) +
    geom_boxplot(fill = NA, color = plot_color, outlier.shape = NA) +
    geom_jitter(color = plot_color, size = 3.5, width = 0.1, alpha = 0.7) +
    scale_x_discrete(drop = FALSE) +
    labs(title = GOI, subtitle = data_source_label, y = y_axis_label, x = NULL) +
    theme_bw(base_size = 16) +
    theme(
      text = element_text(color = "black"), axis.text = element_text(color = "black"),
      axis.ticks = element_line(color = "black"), axis.title.y = element_text(size = 12),
      panel.border = element_blank(), # <<< CHANGE 1: Remove the full border
      axis.line.x = element_line(color = "black"), # <<< CHANGE 2: Add back only the x-axis line
      axis.line.y = element_line(color = "black"), # <<< CHANGE 3: Add back only the y-axis line
      legend.position = "none",
      plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "bold"),
      plot.subtitle = element_text(hjust = 0.5), axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
      panel.grid.major = element_blank(), panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
      plot.margin = margin(t = 20, r = 5, b = 5, l = 5, "pt")
    )

  if (nrow(annotation_df) > 0) {
    p <- p + geom_text(data = annotation_df, aes(x = condition, y = y_pos, label = label), inherit.aes = FALSE, size = star_size, vjust = star_vjust)
  }
  
  min_y <- min(plot_df$expression, na.rm = TRUE)
  max_y_data <- max(plot_df$expression, na.rm = TRUE)
  max_y_annot <- if (nrow(annotation_df) > 0) max(annotation_df$y_pos, na.rm = TRUE) else -Inf
  highest_point <- max(max_y_data, max_y_annot, na.rm = TRUE)
  final_upper_limit <- highest_point + (diff(range(plot_df$expression, na.rm=TRUE)) * 0.15)
  p <- p + coord_cartesian(ylim = c(min_y, final_upper_limit), clip = "off")

  return(p)
}

# --- STEP 4: GENERATE AND PRINT PLOTS FOR EACH GENE ---
plot_colors <- gg_color_hue(2)
my_color_8330 <- plot_colors[1]
my_color_MGH <- plot_colors[2]

for (current_goi in genes_to_plot) {
  message(paste("\n\n<<<<<<<<<< GENERATING PLOTS FOR GENE:", current_goi, ">>>>>>>>>>\n"))

  tryCatch({
  
    p_prot_8330 <- plotAbundance(new_data_imp_8330, current_goi, dep_8330, master_order = master_condition_order, plot_color = my_color_8330, data_source_label = "Proteome - 8330")
    p_prot_MGH <- plotAbundance(new_data_imp_MGH, current_goi, dep_MGH, master_order = master_condition_order, plot_color = my_color_MGH, data_source_label = "Proteome - MGH")
  
    message(paste("\n--- Individual Plots for", current_goi, "---"))
   print(p_prot_8330); print(p_prot_MGH)
    
    message(paste("\n--- Combined RNA-Seq and Proteome Grid for", current_goi, "---"))
    

  }, error = function(e) {
    message(paste("!!! An error occurred while plotting", current_goi, ":", e$message, "!!!"))
  })
  
  message(paste("\n<<<<<<<<<< Finished plotting for", current_goi, ">>>>>>>>>>"))
}
```


MotifMatchR miRNAs
```{r}
# --- Step 1: Load Libraries ---
library(DBI)
library(targetscan.Hs.eg.db)
library(Biostrings)
library(GenomicRanges)
library(BSgenome.Hsapiens.UCSC.hg38)
library(TxDb.Hsapiens.UCSC.hg38.knownGene)
library(org.Hs.eg.db)
library(knitr)
library(tidyverse)


# This script assumes 'foreground_genes_bucket1' and 'background_genes' are pre-defined.


# --- Step 3: Prepare Sequences and Gene Lists ---
message("Fetching 3' UTRs and mapping to gene symbols for Bucket 1...")
utrs <- threeUTRsByTranscript(TxDb.Hsapiens.UCSC.hg38.knownGene, use.names = TRUE)
tx_names <- names(utrs)
tx2geneid <- AnnotationDbi::select(
  TxDb.Hsapiens.UCSC.hg38.knownGene, keys = tx_names,
  keytype = "TXNAME", columns = "GENEID"
)
geneid2symbol <- AnnotationDbi::select(
  org.Hs.eg.db, keys = unique(tx2geneid$GENEID),
  keytype = "ENTREZID", columns = "SYMBOL"
)
tx2symbol <- dplyr::left_join(tx2geneid, geneid2symbol, by = c("GENEID" = "ENTREZID")) %>%
  dplyr::filter(!is.na(SYMBOL) & !is.na(TXNAME))
foreground_tx_names <- tx2symbol %>%
  dplyr::filter(SYMBOL %in% foreground_genes_bucket1) %>%
  dplyr::pull(TXNAME) %>% unique()
background_tx_names <- tx2symbol %>%
  dplyr::filter(SYMBOL %in% background_genes) %>%
  dplyr::pull(TXNAME) %>% unique()
if (length(foreground_tx_names) == 0) {
  stop("Error: None of the foreground gene symbols could be mapped to a transcript.")
}
message("Extracting genomic sequences...")
utrs_foreground <- utrs[names(utrs) %in% foreground_tx_names]
utrs_background <- utrs[names(utrs) %in% background_tx_names]
seqs_foreground_dna <- getSeq(BSgenome.Hsapiens.UCSC.hg38, utrs_foreground)
seqs_background_dna <- getSeq(BSgenome.Hsapiens.UCSC.hg38, utrs_background)


# --- Step 4: Prepare miRNA Motifs from TargetScan ---
message("Preparing miRNA seed region motifs from TargetScan...")
db_path <- system.file("extdata", "targetscan.Hs.eg.sqlite", package = "targetscan.Hs.eg.db")
con <- DBI::dbConnect(RSQLite::SQLite(), db_path)
family_table <- DBI::dbReadTable(con, "mirna_family")
mirbase_table <- DBI::dbReadTable(con, "mirbase")
DBI::dbDisconnect(con)
master_table <- dplyr::inner_join(family_table, mirbase_table, by = c("X_id" = "family"))
mirs_filtered <- master_table %>%
  dplyr::select(miR_Family = name, seed_m8) %>%
  dplyr::distinct()

mir_seeds_dna <- gsub("U", "T", mirs_filtered$seed_m8)
names(mir_seeds_dna) <- mirs_filtered$miR_Family
mir_seeds_dna <- mir_seeds_dna[!is.na(mir_seeds_dna)]
message(paste("Successfully prepared", length(mir_seeds_dna), "unique miRNA motifs for analysis."))


# --- Step 5: Find Motif Matches using Biostrings ---
message("Scanning DNA sequences for miRNA motif matches...")
seqs_foreground_flat <- unlist(seqs_foreground_dna)
seqs_background_flat <- unlist(seqs_background_dna)

foreground_counts_list <- lapply(mir_seeds_dna, function(seed) {
    Biostrings::vcountPattern(pattern = seed, 
                              subject = seqs_foreground_flat, 
                              max.mismatch = 0)
})
motif_hits_foreground <- do.call(cbind, foreground_counts_list)

background_counts_list <- lapply(mir_seeds_dna, function(seed) {
    Biostrings::vcountPattern(pattern = seed, 
                              subject = seqs_background_flat, 
                              max.mismatch = 0)
})
motif_hits_background <- do.call(cbind, background_counts_list)


# --- Step 6: Perform Enrichment Analysis for miRNAs (Bucket 1) ---
message("Performing Fisher's exact test for enrichment...")
final_results_miRNA_bucket1 <- tibble() 
if (ncol(motif_hits_foreground) > 0) {
    foreground_hits <- colSums(motif_hits_foreground > 0)
    background_hits <- colSums(motif_hits_background > 0)
    
    num_foreground_seqs <- nrow(motif_hits_foreground)
    num_background_seqs <- nrow(motif_hits_background)

    fisher_results <- lapply(1:length(mir_seeds_dna), function(i) {
        tbl <- matrix(c(
            foreground_hits[i], background_hits[i],
            num_foreground_seqs - foreground_hits[i],
            num_background_seqs - background_hits[i]
        ), nrow = 2)
        fisher.test(tbl, alternative = "greater")
    })

    final_results_miRNA_bucket1 <- tibble(
        miR_Family = names(mir_seeds_dna),
        p_value = sapply(fisher_results, "[[", "p.value"),
        odds_ratio = sapply(fisher_results, function(x) x$estimate)
    ) %>%
    dplyr::mutate(p_adj = p.adjust(p_value, method = "BH")) %>%
    dplyr::arrange(p_adj)
} else {
    warning("Motif matching returned an empty result.")
}
print("Top 20 enriched miRNA Families for Bucket 1:")
print(head(final_results_miRNA_bucket1, 20))


# --- Step 7: Visualize the Results for miRNAs (Bucket 1) ---


if (nrow(final_results_miRNA_bucket1) > 0) {
  message("Generating final results dot plot for miRNAs (Bucket 1)...")
  
  top_motifs_plot_miRNA_bucket1 <- final_results_miRNA_bucket1 %>%
    # Data preparation steps
    dplyr::filter(is.finite(odds_ratio) & p_adj < 0.05) %>%
    dplyr::top_n(15, -p_adj) %>%
    dplyr::mutate(
      log10_padj = -log10(p_adj),
      miR_Family = fct_reorder(miR_Family, odds_ratio) 
    ) %>%
    
    # ggplot call with aesthetics
    ggplot(aes(x = odds_ratio, y = miR_Family, size = odds_ratio, color = log10_padj)) +
    geom_point(alpha = 0.8) +
    
    # Define the color scale
    scale_color_gradient(low = "lightblue", high = "firebrick") +
    
    # --- CHANGE HERE ---
    # Define the size scale with your four specified breaks
    scale_size_continuous(range = c(4, 10), breaks = c(1.4, 1.6, 1.8, 2.0)) +
    
    # Use labs() to provide distinct titles for each legend
    labs(
      title = "Top 15 Enriched miRNA Motifs in Foreground Genes (Bucket 1)",
      x = "Odds Ratio",
      y = "miRNA Family",
      size = "Odds Ratio",
      color = bquote(-Log[10]~"(p-adj)")
    ) +
    
    # Apply a minimal theme and remove gridlines
    theme_minimal() +
    theme(
      panel.grid.major = element_blank(),
      panel.grid.minor = element_blank()
    )

  print(top_motifs_plot_miRNA_bucket1)
  
} else {
  message("No significant miRNA motifs found to plot for Bucket 1.")
}


# --- Step 8: Print Final Summary Table for miRNAs (Bucket 1) ---
message("--- Final Summary of Enriched miRNA Motifs (p_adj < 0.05) for Bucket 1 ---")
if (nrow(final_results_miRNA_bucket1) > 0) {
  
  summary_table_miRNA_bucket1 <- final_results_miRNA_bucket1 %>%
    dplyr::filter(p_adj < 0.05)
  
  if(nrow(summary_table_miRNA_bucket1) > 0){
    print(knitr::kable(summary_table_miRNA_bucket1, digits = 3, caption = "Enriched miRNA Motifs (Bucket 1)"))
  } else {
    message("No motifs passed the significance threshold of p_adj < 0.05.")
  }
} else {
  message("No enrichment results were generated to create a summary table.")
}
```

Enriched miRNA motifs in bucket 2
```{r}
# --- Step 1: Load Libraries ---
library(DBI)
library(targetscan.Hs.eg.db)
library(Biostrings)
library(GenomicRanges)
library(BSgenome.Hsapiens.UCSC.hg38)
library(TxDb.Hsapiens.UCSC.hg38.knownGene)
library(org.Hs.eg.db)
library(knitr)
library(tidyverse)


# --- Step 3: Prepare Sequences and Gene Lists ---
message("Fetching 3' UTRs and mapping to gene symbols for Bucket 2...")
utrs <- threeUTRsByTranscript(TxDb.Hsapiens.UCSC.hg38.knownGene, use.names = TRUE)
tx_names <- names(utrs)
tx2geneid <- AnnotationDbi::select(
  TxDb.Hsapiens.UCSC.hg38.knownGene, keys = tx_names,
  keytype = "TXNAME", columns = "GENEID"
)
geneid2symbol <- AnnotationDbi::select(
  org.Hs.eg.db, keys = unique(tx2geneid$GENEID),
  keytype = "ENTREZID", columns = "SYMBOL"
)
tx2symbol <- dplyr::left_join(tx2geneid, geneid2symbol, by = c("GENEID" = "ENTREZID")) %>%
  dplyr::filter(!is.na(SYMBOL) & !is.na(TXNAME))
foreground_tx_names <- tx2symbol %>%
  dplyr::filter(SYMBOL %in% foreground_genes_bucket2) %>%
  dplyr::pull(TXNAME) %>% unique()
background_tx_names <- tx2symbol %>%
  dplyr::filter(SYMBOL %in% background_genes) %>%
  dplyr::pull(TXNAME) %>% unique()
if (length(foreground_tx_names) == 0) {
  stop("Error: None of the foreground gene symbols could be mapped to a transcript.")
}
message("Extracting genomic sequences...")
utrs_foreground <- utrs[names(utrs) %in% foreground_tx_names]
utrs_background <- utrs[names(utrs) %in% background_tx_names]
seqs_foreground_dna <- getSeq(BSgenome.Hsapiens.UCSC.hg38, utrs_foreground)
seqs_background_dna <- getSeq(BSgenome.Hsapiens.UCSC.hg38, utrs_background)


# --- Step 4: Prepare miRNA Motifs from TargetScan ---
message("Preparing miRNA seed region motifs from TargetScan...")
db_path <- system.file("extdata", "targetscan.Hs.eg.sqlite", package = "targetscan.Hs.eg.db")
con <- DBI::dbConnect(RSQLite::SQLite(), db_path)
family_table <- DBI::dbReadTable(con, "mirna_family")
mirbase_table <- DBI::dbReadTable(con, "mirbase")
DBI::dbDisconnect(con)
master_table <- dplyr::inner_join(family_table, mirbase_table, by = c("X_id" = "family"))
mirs_filtered <- master_table %>%
  dplyr::select(miR_Family = name, seed_m8) %>%
  dplyr::distinct()
mir_seeds_dna <- gsub("U", "T", mirs_filtered$seed_m8)
names(mir_seeds_dna) <- mirs_filtered$miR_Family
mir_seeds_dna <- mir_seeds_dna[!is.na(mir_seeds_dna)]
message(paste("Successfully prepared", length(mir_seeds_dna), "unique miRNA motifs for analysis."))


# --- Step 5: Find Motif Matches using Biostrings ---
message("Scanning DNA sequences for miRNA motif matches...")
seqs_foreground_flat <- unlist(seqs_foreground_dna)
seqs_background_flat <- unlist(seqs_background_dna)

foreground_counts_list <- lapply(mir_seeds_dna, function(seed) {
    Biostrings::vcountPattern(pattern = seed, 
                              subject = seqs_foreground_flat, 
                              max.mismatch = 0)
})
motif_hits_foreground <- do.call(cbind, foreground_counts_list)

background_counts_list <- lapply(mir_seeds_dna, function(seed) {
    Biostrings::vcountPattern(pattern = seed, 
                              subject = seqs_background_flat, 
                              max.mismatch = 0)
})
motif_hits_background <- do.call(cbind, background_counts_list)


# --- Step 6: Perform Enrichment Analysis for miRNAs (Bucket 2) ---
message("Performing Fisher's exact test for enrichment...")
final_results_miRNA_bucket2 <- tibble() 
if (ncol(motif_hits_foreground) > 0) {
    foreground_hits <- colSums(motif_hits_foreground > 0)
    background_hits <- colSums(motif_hits_background > 0)
    
    num_foreground_seqs <- nrow(motif_hits_foreground)
    num_background_seqs <- nrow(motif_hits_background)

    fisher_results <- lapply(1:length(mir_seeds_dna), function(i) {
        tbl <- matrix(c(
            foreground_hits[i], background_hits[i],
            num_foreground_seqs - foreground_hits[i],
            num_background_seqs - background_hits[i]
        ), nrow = 2)
        fisher.test(tbl, alternative = "greater")
    })

    final_results_miRNA_bucket2 <- tibble(
        miR_Family = names(mir_seeds_dna),
        p_value = sapply(fisher_results, "[[", "p.value"),
        odds_ratio = sapply(fisher_results, function(x) x$estimate)
    ) %>%
    dplyr::mutate(p_adj = p.adjust(p_value, method = "BH")) %>%
    dplyr::arrange(p_adj)
} else {
    warning("Motif matching returned an empty result.")
}
print("Top 20 enriched miRNA Families for Bucket 2:")
print(head(final_results_miRNA_bucket2, 20))


# --- Step 7: Visualize the Results for miRNAs (Bucket 2) ---

if (nrow(final_results_miRNA_bucket2) > 0) {
  message("Generating results dot plot for miRNAs (Bucket 2)...")
  
  top_motifs_plot_miRNA_bucket2 <- final_results_miRNA_bucket2 %>%
    # Data preparation steps
    dplyr::filter(is.finite(odds_ratio) & p_adj < 0.05) %>%
    
    # --- TYPO FIX HERE ---
    dplyr::top_n(15, -p_adj) %>% # Corrected from -p-adj
    
    dplyr::mutate(
      log10_padj = -log10(p_adj),
      miR_Family = fct_reorder(miR_Family, odds_ratio) 
    ) %>%
    
    # ggplot call with updated aesthetics
    ggplot(aes(x = odds_ratio, y = miR_Family, size = odds_ratio, color = log10_padj)) +
    geom_point(alpha = 0.8) +
    
    # Define the color scale
    scale_color_gradient(low = "lightblue", high = "firebrick") +
    
    # Define the size scale with your specified breaks
    # Note: You may need to adjust these breaks for the Bucket 2 data range
    scale_size_continuous(range = c(4, 10), breaks = c(1.4, 1.6, 1.8, 2.0)) +
    
    # Use labs() to provide distinct titles for each legend
    labs(
      title = "Top 15 Enriched miRNA Motifs in Foreground Genes (Bucket 2)",
      x = "Odds Ratio",
      y = "miRNA Family",
      size = "Odds Ratio",
      color = bquote(-Log[10]~"(p-adj)")
    ) +
    
    # Apply a minimal theme and remove gridlines
    theme_minimal() +
    theme(
      panel.grid.major = element_blank(),
      panel.grid.minor = element_blank()
    )

  print(top_motifs_plot_miRNA_bucket2)
  
} else {
  message("No significant miRNA motifs found to plot for Bucket 2.")
}


# --- Step 8: Print Final Summary Table for miRNAs (Bucket 2) ---
message("--- Final Summary of Enriched miRNA Motifs (p_adj < 0.05) for Bucket 2 ---")
if (nrow(final_results_miRNA_bucket2) > 0) {
  
  summary_table_miRNA_bucket2 <- final_results_miRNA_bucket2 %>%
    dplyr::filter(p_adj < 0.05)
  
  if(nrow(summary_table_miRNA_bucket2) > 0){
    print(knitr::kable(summary_table_miRNA_bucket2, digits = 3, caption = "Enriched miRNA Motifs (Bucket 2)"))
  } else {
    message("No motifs passed the significance threshold of p_adj < 0.05.")
  }
} else {
  message("No enrichment results were generated to create a summary table.")
}
```


```{r}
# --- Step 11: Overlap Bucket 1 and Bucket 2 miRNA Results ---
# Make sure the VennDiagram package is installed: install.packages("VennDiagram")
library(VennDiagram)
library(dplyr)
library(grid) # Required for grid.newpage() and grid.draw()

message("--- Comparing Enriched miRNA Families from Bucket 1 and Bucket 2 ---")

# 1. Extract the unique names of significantly enriched miRNA families from each bucket
bucket1_miRNAs <- final_results_miRNA_bucket1 %>%
  dplyr::filter(p_adj < 0.05) %>%
  dplyr::pull(miR_Family) %>%
  unique()

bucket2_miRNAs <- final_results_miRNA_bucket2 %>%
  dplyr::filter(p_adj < 0.05) %>%
  dplyr::pull(miR_Family) %>%
  unique()

# 2. Prepare the data for the Venn diagram
venn_list_miRNA <- list(
  "Bucket 1" = bucket1_miRNAs,
  "Bucket 2" = bucket2_miRNAs
)

# 3. Create and draw the Venn diagram
# This clears any previous plots
grid.newpage() 

# Generate the plot object
# Load the necessary library
library(VennDiagram)

# Your list of items for the Venn diagram
# venn_list_miRNA <- list(Bucket1 = ..., Bucket2 = ...)

# --- Corrected Plotting Code ---
venn.diagram(
  x = venn_list_miRNA,
  
  # 1. Set a filename
  filename = 'venn_plot.png',
  output = TRUE,
  
  # 2. Set equal width and height for a square canvas
  imagetype="png" ,
  height = 2000, 
  width = 2000, 
  resolution = 300,
  compression = "lzw",
  
  # --- Style Options ---
  col = c("seagreen", "darkorange"),
  fill = c(alpha("seagreen", 0.3), alpha("darkorange", 0.3)),
  cex = 1.5,
  fontfamily = "sans",
  cat.cex = 1.2,
  cat.fontfamily = "sans",
  cat.col = c("seagreen", "darkorange")
)

# Draw the plot to the active graphics device
grid.draw(venn_plot_miRNA)


# 4. List the common and unique miRNA families
common_miRNAs <- intersect(bucket1_miRNAs, bucket2_miRNAs)
unique_to_bucket1_miRNAs <- setdiff(bucket1_miRNAs, bucket2_miRNAs)
unique_to_bucket2_miRNAs <- setdiff(bucket2_miRNAs, bucket1_miRNAs)

cat("\n--- Summary of miRNA Overlap ---\n")
cat("\nmiRNA Families common to BOTH buckets:", paste(common_miRNAs, collapse=", "), "\n")
cat("\nmiRNA Families unique to Bucket 1:", paste(unique_to_bucket1_miRNAs, collapse=", "), "\n")
cat("\nmiRNA Families unique to Bucket 2:", paste(unique_to_bucket2_miRNAs, collapse=", "), "\n")
```

```{r}
# --- Step 11: Overlap Bucket 1 and Bucket 2 miRNA Results ---

# --- MODIFIED LIBRARIES ---
# Make sure the eulerr package is installed: install.packages("eulerr")
library(eulerr)     # For plotting area-proportional Euler diagrams
library(grid)       # For drawing the plot grob
library(dplyr)

message("--- Comparing Enriched miRNA Families from Bucket 1 and Bucket 2 ---")

# --- Pre-flight Check ---
# Ensure the necessary result tables exist
if (exists("final_results_miRNA_bucket1") && exists("final_results_miRNA_bucket2")) {

  # 1. Extract the unique names of significantly enriched miRNA families from each bucket
  bucket1_miRNAs <- final_results_miRNA_bucket1 %>%
    dplyr::filter(p_adj < 0.05) %>%
    dplyr::pull(miR_Family) %>%
    unique()

  bucket2_miRNAs <- final_results_miRNA_bucket2 %>%
    dplyr::filter(p_adj < 0.05) %>%
    dplyr::pull(miR_Family) %>%
    unique()
    
  message(paste("Found", length(bucket1_miRNAs), "significant families in Bucket 1."))
  message(paste("Found", length(bucket2_miRNAs), "significant families in Bucket 2."))

  # 2. Prepare the data for the Euler diagram
  venn_list_miRNA <- list(
    "Bucket 1" = bucket1_miRNAs,
    "Bucket 2" = bucket2_miRNAs
  )

  # --- MODIFIED PLOTTING STEP ---
  # 3. Create and draw the Euler diagram
  message("\n--- Generating Euler Diagram ---")
  grid.newpage() # Clear the plot device
  
  # Create the euler plot object
  # quantities = TRUE adds the numbers to the segments
  euler_plot_grob <- plot(euler(venn_list_miRNA), quantities = TRUE)
  
  # Draw the plot to the graphics device
  grid.draw(euler_plot_grob)
  # --- END OF PLOTTING MODIFICATION ---


  # 4. List the common and unique miRNA families
  common_miRNAs <- intersect(bucket1_miRNAs, bucket2_miRNAs)
  unique_to_bucket1_miRNAs <- setdiff(bucket1_miRNAs, bucket2_miRNAs)
  unique_to_bucket2_miRNAs <- setdiff(bucket2_miRNAs, bucket1_miRNAs)

  cat("\n--- Summary of miRNA Overlap ---\n")
  cat("Total overlapping families:", length(common_miRNAs), "\n")
  cat("\nFamilies common to BOTH buckets:", paste(common_miRNAs, collapse=", "), "\n")
  cat("\nFamilies unique to Bucket 1:", paste(unique_to_bucket1_miRNAs, collapse=", "), "\n")
  cat("\nFamilies unique to Bucket 2:", paste(unique_to_bucket2_miRNAs, collapse=", "), "\n")
  
  
  # --- NEW STATISTICAL ANALYSIS STEP ---
  # 5. Perform the Hypergeometric Test
  message("\n--- Calculating Statistical Significance of Overlap ---")
  
  # --- Define the Statistical Universe ---
  # We define the universe as all unique miRNA families present in EITHER 
  # of the full (unfiltered) results tables. This represents all families
  # that were tested and could have been found significant.
  
  all_b1_families <- unique(final_results_miRNA_bucket1$miR_Family)
  all_b2_families <- unique(final_results_miRNA_bucket2$miR_Family)
  total_mirna_universe <- union(all_b1_families, all_b2_families)
  
  if(length(total_mirna_universe) == 0) {
    message("Error: Universe size is zero. Cannot perform hypergeometric test.")
  } else {
    message(paste("Statistical universe defined as all families tested:", length(total_mirna_universe)))

    # Define the four numbers for the test:
    q <- length(common_miRNAs) # Overlap
    m <- length(bucket1_miRNAs) # Size of List 1 (significant in B1)
    k <- length(bucket2_miRNAs) # Size of List 2 (significant in B2)
    n <- length(total_mirna_universe) - m # All non-List 1 items in universe

    # Calculate the p-value for the overlap
    p_value <- phyper(q - 1, m, n, k, lower.tail = FALSE)

    cat("--- Test Parameters --- \n")
    cat("Overlap size (q):", q, "\n")
    cat("Size of List 1 'm' (Bucket 1):", m, "\n")
    cat("Size of List 2 'k' (Bucket 2):", k, "\n")
    cat("Size of Universe 'm+n':", m + n, "\n")
    cat("---------------------- \n")
    cat("Hypergeometric test p-value:", format.pval(p_value, digits = 3), "\n")

    if (p_value < 0.05) {
      cat("Result: The overlap between Bucket 1 and Bucket 2 enriched miRNA families is STATISTICALLY SIGNIFICANT.\n")
    } else {
      cat("Result: The overlap is NOT statistically significant.\n")
    }
  }
  
} else {
  message("Error: 'final_results_miRNA_bucket1' or 'final_results_miRNA_bucket2' not found. Cannot perform overlap analysis.")
}
```



Export results from ridgeline plots
```{r}
# Load necessary library
library(openxlsx)
library(dplyr)

# ==============================================================================
# EXPORT: AVERAGED TRIPLICATES 
# ==============================================================================

# 1. Prepare and Combine Raw Data
mgh_ratio_data$Background <- "MGH"
h8330_ratio_data$Background <- "8330"

raw_combined <- rbind(mgh_ratio_data, h8330_ratio_data)

# 2. Average the Triplicates
# We group by Gene, Background, and Genotype to calculate the mean across replicates
averaged_table <- raw_combined %>%
  group_by(Gene, Background, condition) %>%
  summarise(
    Mean_Protein_Abundance_Log2 = mean(Protein_Abundance, na.rm = TRUE),
    Mean_RNA_Abundance_VST = mean(RNA_Abundance, na.rm = TRUE),
    Mean_Protein_RNA_Log2_Ratio = mean(Log2Ratio, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  arrange(condition, Background, Gene) # Sort for clarity

# 3. Initialize Workbook
wb <- createWorkbook()
header_style <- createStyle(textDecoration = "bold", halign = "center")

# 4. Loop Logic: Create sheets for Genotype + Background combinations
unique_genotypes <- unique(averaged_table$condition)
unique_backgrounds <- c("MGH", "8330")

for (geno in unique_genotypes) {
  for (bg in unique_backgrounds) {
    
    # A. Filter data for this specific Genotype AND Background
    sheet_data <- averaged_table %>% 
      filter(condition == geno, Background == bg) %>%
      dplyr::select(-condition, -Background) 
    
    if(nrow(sheet_data) > 0) {
      
      sheet_name <- paste0(geno, "_", bg)
      
      sheet_name <- substr(sheet_name, 1, 31)
      
      addWorksheet(wb, sheet_name)
      
      writeData(wb, sheet = sheet_name, x = sheet_data)
      
      addStyle(wb, sheet = sheet_name, style = header_style, rows = 1, cols = 1:ncol(sheet_data))
      setColWidths(wb, sheet = sheet_name, cols = 1:ncol(sheet_data), widths = "auto")
    }
  }
}

# 5. Save
saveWorkbook(wb, "Supplementary_Table_Protein_RNA_Ratios_Averaged.xlsx", overwrite = TRUE)

cat("\nSuccess! 'Supplementary_Table_Protein_RNA_Ratios_Averaged.xlsx' created.")
cat("\nTriplicates have been averaged. Sheets are organized by Genotype_Background.")
```


Export lists of bucket 1 and 2
```{r}
# Load necessary libraries
library(dplyr)
library(openxlsx)

# ==============================================================================
# EXPORT: OVERLAPPING PROTEOME-SPECIFIC GENES (SIMPLE LIST)
# ==============================================================================

# --- 1. Define Bucket 1 (UP) Intersection ---
# Filter all genes fitting the criteria
bucket1_all <- master_integrated_table %>%
  dplyr::filter(
    Protein_padj < 0.05 & Protein_Log2FC > 0,
    !(mRNA_padj < 0.05 & mRNA_Log2FC > 0)
  )

# Split by comparison and find the intersection (genes present in ALL lists)
bucket1_list <- bucket1_all %>% group_split(comparison)
bucket1_vectors <- lapply(bucket1_list, function(df) df$Gene)
bucket1_overlap <- Reduce(intersect, bucket1_vectors)

# --- 2. Define Bucket 2 (DOWN) Intersection ---
# Filter all genes fitting the criteria
bucket2_all <- master_integrated_table %>%
  dplyr::filter(
    Protein_padj < 0.05 & Protein_Log2FC < 0,
    !(mRNA_padj < 0.05 & mRNA_Log2FC < 0)
  )

# Split by comparison and find the intersection
bucket2_list <- bucket2_all %>% group_split(comparison)
bucket2_vectors <- lapply(bucket2_list, function(df) df$Gene)
bucket2_overlap <- Reduce(intersect, bucket2_vectors)

# --- 3. Prepare Data Frames for Export ---
# Convert the vectors to dataframes so they can be written to Excel
df_bucket1 <- data.frame(Gene = bucket1_overlap)
df_bucket2 <- data.frame(Gene = bucket2_overlap)

# --- 4. Create and Save Excel File ---
wb <- createWorkbook()
header_style <- createStyle(textDecoration = "bold", halign = "center")

# Sheet 1: Proteome-Spec Upregulation
sheet1 <- "Proteome-Spec Upregulation"
addWorksheet(wb, sheet1)
writeData(wb, sheet1, df_bucket1)
addStyle(wb, sheet1, header_style, rows = 1, cols = 1)

# Sheet 2: Proteome-Spec Downregulation
sheet2 <- "Proteome-Spec Downregulation"
addWorksheet(wb, sheet2)
writeData(wb, sheet2, df_bucket2)
addStyle(wb, sheet2, header_style, rows = 1, cols = 1)

# Save
saveWorkbook(wb, "Supplementary_Table_Core_Proteome_Discordance.xlsx", overwrite = TRUE)

cat("\nSuccess! 'Supplementary_Table_Core_Proteome_Discordance.xlsx' created.")
cat("\nProteome-Specific UP Genes (Intersection):", length(bucket1_overlap))
cat("\nProteome-Specific DOWN Genes (Intersection):", length(bucket2_overlap))
```


```{r}
# --- Step 2: Convert Gene Symbols to Entrez IDs ---
foreground_genes_bucket1_entrez <- bitr(
  foreground_genes_bucket1, 
  fromType = "SYMBOL", 
  toType = "ENTREZID", 
  OrgDb = "org.Hs.eg.db"
)

# Check the conversion
print(head(foreground_genes_bucket1_entrez))

# --- Step 3: Run GO Enrichment Analysis ---
bucket2_enrich <- enrichGO(
  gene          = foreground_genes_bucket1_entrez$ENTREZID,  # <--- FIX: Select the column
  keyType       = "ENTREZID",
  OrgDb         = org.Hs.eg.db,
  ont           = "ALL",        # Runs BP, CC, and MF. Use "BP" for just Biological Process.
  pAdjustMethod = "BH",
  pvalueCutoff  = 0.05,
  qvalueCutoff  = 0.05,
  readable      = TRUE          # Maps IDs back to Symbols in the output
)

# --- Step 4: View Results ---
if (!is.null(bucket1_enrich)) {
  print(head(bucket1_enrich))
} else {
  print("No significant GO terms found. Try increasing pvalueCutoff to 0.1 or 0.2 to check for weak signal.")
}
```



```{r}
# --- Step 2: Convert Gene Symbols to Entrez IDs ---
foreground_genes_bucket2_entrez <- bitr(
  foreground_genes_bucket2, 
  fromType = "SYMBOL", 
  toType = "ENTREZID", 
  OrgDb = "org.Hs.eg.db"
)

# Check the conversion
print(head(foreground_genes_bucket2_entrez))

# --- Step 3: Run GO Enrichment Analysis ---
bucket2_enrich <- enrichGO(
  gene          = foreground_genes_bucket2_entrez$ENTREZID,  # <--- FIX: Select the column
  keyType       = "ENTREZID",
  OrgDb         = org.Hs.eg.db,
  ont           = "ALL",        # Runs BP, CC, and MF. Use "BP" for just Biological Process.
  pAdjustMethod = "BH",
  pvalueCutoff  = 0.05,
  qvalueCutoff  = 0.05,
  readable      = TRUE          # Maps IDs back to Symbols in the output
)

# --- Step 4: View Results ---
if (!is.null(bucket2_enrich)) {
  print(head(bucket2_enrich))
} else {
  print("No significant GO terms found. Try increasing pvalueCutoff to 0.1 or 0.2 to check for weak signal.")
}
```



Heatmap of RBP-enriched proteins
```{r}
suppressPackageStartupMessages({
  library(SummarizedExperiment)
  library(pheatmap)
  library(RColorBrewer)
})

# ----------------------------
# 0) Inputs
# ----------------------------
se_mgh  <- data_imp_MGH_new
se_8330 <- data_imp_8330_new

proteins_to_plot <- c(
  "GLYR1","CHD2","ACO1","RPL35","HSPA5","HNRNPAO","AHCTF1","RBBP5",
  "R3HDM2","SND1","TSNAX","RAN","RFC3","NELFB","PSMC2","CEBPZ"
)

# ----------------------------
# Helpers
# ----------------------------
unwrap_se <- function(x) {
  if (inherits(x, "SummarizedExperiment")) return(x)
  if (is.list(x)) {
    idx <- which(vapply(x, inherits, logical(1), what="SummarizedExperiment"))[1]
    if (!is.na(idx)) return(x[[idx]])
  }
  stop("Could not unwrap into SummarizedExperiment")
}

get_assay_matrix <- function(se) {
  se <- unwrap_se(se)

  an <- assayNames(se)
  if (length(an) == 0) {
    mat <- assay(se, 1)
  } else {
    preferred <- c("imputed","Imputed","log2","intensity","abundance","exprs","counts")
    use <- preferred[preferred %in% an]
    if (length(use) == 0) use <- an[1] else use <- use[1]
    mat <- assay(se, use)
  }
  mat <- as.matrix(mat)
  mode(mat) <- "numeric"
  mat
}

get_symbol_map <- function(se) {
  se <- unwrap_se(se)
  rd <- as.data.frame(rowData(se))

  candidate_symbol_cols <- c("name","Gene","gene","gene_name","SYMBOL","Symbol","Protein","protein","PG.Genes")
  symbol_col <- candidate_symbol_cols[candidate_symbol_cols %in% colnames(rd)][1]

  sym <- if (!is.na(symbol_col)) as.character(rd[[symbol_col]]) else rownames(se)

  m <- setNames(seq_along(sym), sym)
  m <- m[!duplicated(names(m))]
  m
}

cal_z_row <- function(mat) t(scale(t(mat)))

gg_color_hue <- function(n) {
  hues <- seq(15, 375, length = n + 1)
  hcl(h = hues, l = 65, c = 100)[1:n]
}

# ----------------------------
# 1) Extract matrices and subset proteins
# ----------------------------
se_mgh  <- unwrap_se(se_mgh)
se_8330 <- unwrap_se(se_8330)

mat_mgh_raw  <- get_assay_matrix(se_mgh)
mat_8330_raw <- get_assay_matrix(se_8330)

map_mgh  <- get_symbol_map(se_mgh)
map_8330 <- get_symbol_map(se_8330)

valid_proteins <- intersect(proteins_to_plot, intersect(names(map_mgh), names(map_8330)))
if (length(valid_proteins) < 2) stop("Fewer than 2 proteins found in BOTH backgrounds.")

mat_mgh_sub  <- mat_mgh_raw[ unname(map_mgh[valid_proteins]),  , drop=FALSE]
mat_8330_sub <- mat_8330_raw[unname(map_8330[valid_proteins]), , drop=FALSE]

rownames(mat_mgh_sub)  <- valid_proteins
rownames(mat_8330_sub) <- valid_proteins

# ----------------------------
# 2) IMPORTANT FIX: make sample names unique BEFORE combining
# ----------------------------
colnames(mat_mgh_sub)  <- paste0("MGH_",  colnames(mat_mgh_sub))
colnames(mat_8330_sub) <- paste0("8330_", colnames(mat_8330_sub))

# ----------------------------
# 3) Z-score separately by background, recombine
# ----------------------------
z_mgh  <- cal_z_row(mat_mgh_sub)
z_8330 <- cal_z_row(mat_8330_sub)
z_matrix_combined <- cbind(z_mgh, z_8330)

# ----------------------------
# 4) Column annotations (genotype/condition + background) and sorting
# ----------------------------
col_anno_mgh  <- as.data.frame(colData(se_mgh))
col_anno_8330 <- as.data.frame(colData(se_8330))

# rownames must match the NEW prefixed sample names
rownames(col_anno_mgh)  <- colnames(z_mgh)   # MGH_* names
rownames(col_anno_8330) <- colnames(z_8330)  # 8330_* names

col_anno_mgh$background  <- "MGH"
col_anno_8330$background <- "8330"

# your "genotype" is stored as condition
col_anno_mgh$genotype  <- as.character(col_anno_mgh$condition)
col_anno_8330$genotype <- as.character(col_anno_8330$condition)

col_annotation_all <- rbind(col_anno_mgh, col_anno_8330)
col_annotation_all <- col_annotation_all[colnames(z_matrix_combined), , drop=FALSE]

# strict factor levels (same as your RNA heatmap)
col_annotation_all$genotype <- factor(
  col_annotation_all$genotype,
  levels = c("DelC","DupC","HetDel","HomoDel","PWSTI","PWSTII","WT")
)
col_annotation_all$background <- factor(
  col_annotation_all$background,
  levels = c("MGH","8330")
)

# sort columns genotype -> background (same style)
col_annotation_sorted <- col_annotation_all[
  order(col_annotation_all$genotype, col_annotation_all$background), , drop=FALSE
]
ordered_samples <- rownames(col_annotation_sorted)

# final matrix in sorted order
heatmap_matrix_final <- z_matrix_combined[, ordered_samples, drop=FALSE]

# ----------------------------
# 5) Colors + pheatmap (match your RNA style)
# ----------------------------
genotype_colors <- setNames(
  gg_color_hue(length(levels(col_annotation_sorted$genotype))),
  levels(col_annotation_sorted$genotype)
)
background_colors <- setNames(
  gg_color_hue(length(levels(col_annotation_sorted$background))),
  levels(col_annotation_sorted$background)
)
ann_colors_final <- list(
  genotype = genotype_colors,
  background = background_colors
)

pheatmap(
  heatmap_matrix_final,

  annotation_col = col_annotation_sorted[, c("genotype","background"), drop=FALSE],
  annotation_colors = ann_colors_final,

  cluster_rows = TRUE,
  cluster_cols = FALSE,

  show_rownames = TRUE,
  show_colnames = TRUE,
  fontsize_row = 8,
  fontsize_col = 8,

  color = colorRampPalette(rev(brewer.pal(n = 10, name = "RdBu")))(100),
  main = "Proteomics (MGH + 8330): Selected Proteins (Z-score by Background)",
  scale = "none",
  breaks = seq(-2, 2, length.out = 101),
  border_color = NA
)
```
